title: Bank Loan Privacy Act
plain_summary: The Bank Loan Privacy Act is about protecting information from small business loans. It says that the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection must follow special rules before they can delete or change any small business loan information. Before making any changes, the Bureau has to create a public rule that explains exactly what they want to change and why. They also have to show how their changes will keep people's private information safe. This law makes sure the Bureau cannot secretly change important loan information without telling the public first.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Bank Loan Privacy Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Rulemaking Requirement
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Equal Credit Opportunity Act. The change affects how the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection handles information about small business loans. Currently, the Bureau can delete or change this loan information whenever it wants. This new rule requires the Bureau to follow a specific process before making any changes. The Bureau must create a new rule and let the public comment on it first. This new rule must explain exactly what information the Bureau plans to delete or change. It must also explain how these changes will help protect people's privacy. The Bureau cannot make any changes to the loan information until this public rule-making process is complete.
